known_breaks.reason: Concurrent PostToolUse audit-log.sh invocations (Claude Code firing parallel tool calls in one turn) raced on a read-tail-then-append with no lock — a fork, not tampering. Root cause fixed in .claude/hooks/audit-log.sh (mkdir-based lock) on 2026-07-16, found live by giamthi-watch.sh's first real run. A full scan of audit-chain.log (2026-07-16) found 8 occurrences of this same signature between 2026-07-09 and 2026-07-13, all listed in this file; none after the fix. `expected_prev` is the hash the verifier had genuinely computed by walking the real chain up to this exact point, at the time this exception was reviewed and recorded (2026-07-16) — required in addition to ts+hash so this exception cannot be replayed against a forged prefix (see verify-audit-chain.sh's is_known_break()).
known_breaks.reviewed: 2026-07-16, code-auditor (fixed after a real replay-bypass this exact allowlist mechanism was found to have, in an earlier version keyed on ts+hash alone)
